title: [DEV BUILD] DuckDB Crash with Correlated Aggregation inside a WITH clause
description:
### What happens?

Hi! I found the following query will cause `SIGSEGV (Address boundary error)` from DuckDB.




### To Reproduce

Run the following statements in DuckDB CLI
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 BOOLEAN);

SELECT * FROM t0
WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM t1
  WHERE (WITH seq(i) AS (VALUES (1)) SELECT SUM(i) * t0.c0 FROM seq) IS NOT NULL
);
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS, x86_64, Linux 6.14.0-1010-aws (AWS EC2)

### DuckDB Version:

v1.4.0-dev2739 (Development Version)

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Qiuyang Mang

### Affiliation:

UC Berkeley

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(c0 Int32, c1 Float64, c2 UInt8);
CREATE TABLE t3(c0 String, c1 Date);
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t3 WHERE (SELECT SUM(i) * COALESCE(t2.c1, 0) FROM (SELECT 1 AS i UNION ALL SELECT 2) AS seq) IS NOT NULL);
key_bug_features: Correlated aggregation inside a WITH clause
